herd is 5 elephants smaller than the second herd.] [This means we subtract 5 from the number of elephants in the second herd.] [24 minus 5 equals 19.] [So, the third herd has 19 elephants.] [Now, we need to calculate the total number of elephants at the waterhole.] [We add the number of elephants in all three herds together.] [The first herd has 12 elephants.] [The second herd has 24 elephants.] [The third herd has 19 elephants.] [Adding these numbers together gives us the total.] [12 plus 24 equals 36.] [36 plus 19 equals 55.] [Therefore, there are 55 elephants in total at the waterhole.] [This is a significant gathering of elephants.] [The indigenous community member is impressed by the size of the group.] [They have observed this phenomenon before, but it is not very common.] [Such
